message:
fix(crdt): ORSet tombstone tracking prevents removed values from reappearing on merge

The ORSet (Observed-Remove Set) CRDT had a correctness bug where
remove() simply deleted all tags from the entries map, but merge()
would reintroduce those same tags from a stale replica that hadn't
yet observed the remove.  This violates the fundamental ORSet
contract: a remove must win over any concurrent add that uses an
already-observed tag.

Fix: ORSet now maintains a tombstones BTreeSet.  remove() moves
tags from entries into tombstones instead of discarding them.
merge() checks each incoming tag against tombstones before
inserting it.  This ensures that once a tag is tombstoned, it
can never be reintroduced by a lagging replica.

The tombstone set grows monotonically, which is standard for
ORSet implementations — garbage collection of tombstones requires
causal stability (all replicas have observed the remove), which
is outside the scope of this fix.

New regression test: tests/orset_bug.rs —
orset_remove_is_not_undone_by_old_replica exercises the exact
scenario: add on A, clone to B, remove on A, merge B back into A,
assert value stays removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
